title: Add framer-motion animations to all 11 inline canvas visualizations
description:
VA2 (Animation) scores 0/3 for 8 of 11 canvases and 1/3 for the remaining 3. Only CRDT has real animation (3/3). Meanwhile, the orphaned visualizers and Learn panel both use framer-motion with proper easing.

The architectural fix is: when DIS-024 (god file split) extracts canvases to separate files, convert them from raw SVG to framer-motion animated SVG. Key animations to add:

1. RaftCanvas: Role transition (color fade over 300ms instead of instant), leader glow pulse, message dots with easeOut instead of linear
2. ConsistentHashCanvas: Spring animation for new keys (from center to ring position), spring for new nodes, fade for removed keys
3. GossipCanvas: Gossip arrow fly animation (similar to Raft messages but with easeOut)
4. CAPCanvas: Partition line slide-in animation, node state transitions
5. VectorClockCanvas/LamportCanvas: Event dot fade-in on creation
6. TwoPCCanvas/SagaCanvas/PaxosCanvas: Step transitions with fade
7. MapReduceCanvas: Phase transition animation (pipeline stages highlight sequentially)

Reference implementations: RaftVisualizer.tsx:144 (easeOut 0.4s), ConsistentHashRingVisualizer.tsx:177 (spring stiffness 150 damping 18).
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 canvases use framer-motion for state transitions
No instant color/position changes — minimum 200ms transition
Raft message dots use easeOut instead of linear
New elements enter with fade or spring, not instant pop
60fps maintained (no jank during animation)

title: Unify visual language: shared animation config, easing curves, timing, and node components
description:
Three different visual languages coexist: (1) Main canvases: no animation, hardcoded colors, (2) Learn panel: framer-motion + CSS vars, (3) Orphaned visualizers: framer-motion + IBM palette. Cross-module consistency score: 2/10.

Create shared config at src/lib/visualization/distributed-config.ts:
- Shared easing: spring({ damping: 20, stiffness: 300 }) for snappy, spring({ damping: 25, stiffness: 150 }) for smooth
- Shared durations: 200ms state change, 400ms movement, 300ms fade
- Shared color tokens: role-leader, role-follower, role-candidate, state-active, state-inactive, state-error
- Shared <SimNode> component: consistent size, stroke, hover, glow
- Shared <MessageArrow> component: consistent fly animation, color by type
acceptanceCriteria: Shared animation config file exists with documented constants
All 11 canvases import from shared config
Same easing for same transition type across all canvases
Same color tokens for equivalent states
A node in Raft looks/feels like a node in Gossip/CAP

title: Replace linear Raft message animation with easeOut and add role transition animation
description:
RaftCanvas message dots use pure linear interpolation at DistributedModule.tsx:251: (now - m.startTime) / ANIMATION_DURATION_MS. Dots move at constant speed — robotic, not organic. Orphaned RaftVisualizer uses transition={{ duration: 0.4, ease: 'easeOut' }} at line 144.

Also: node role transitions (follower→candidate→leader) are INSTANT color changes. Orphaned version has transition={{ duration: 0.3 }} and a pulsing glow ring for leader (lines 189-199).

Fix: easeOut message dots, 300ms role transition, leader glow pulse, crashed fade-out.
acceptanceCriteria: Message dots use easeOut (decelerate at end)
Role transitions fade over 300ms
Leader has pulsing glow ring
Crashed nodes fade to opacity 0.3 over 200ms

title: Add spring-physics key placement and node entry animation to Consistent Hashing
description:
ConsistentHashCanvas has ZERO animation (VA2=0/3). Orphaned ConsistentHashRingVisualizer has spring animation: transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 150, damping: 18 }} at line 177 for keys, and transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 200, damping: 15 }} at line 201 for nodes.

Add: keys spring from center to ring position, nodes spring into place, removed elements fade out. Reference: selfboot.cn/algorithms/hashring shows best interactive ring with color-coded segments updating in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: New keys animate from center to ring position with spring physics
New nodes spring into place on the ring perimeter
Removed keys fade out over 200ms
Removed nodes fade + collapse their ring segment

title: Add hover interactions and tooltips to all node-based visualizations
description:
No canvas has hover states — only SVG <title> tooltip (no styling control). No canvas has interactive cursor feedback. Compare: TopologyAwareFailureModes has whileTap={{ scale: 0.97 }}, SplitBrainVisualizer has animate={{ scale: [1, 1.05, 1] }}.

For all node-based canvases (Raft, Gossip, CAP, 2PC, Paxos, Consistent Hashing): nodes scale 1.05 on hover with styled tooltip showing node details (role, term, data, state). Reference: Bartosz Ciechanowski (ciechanow.ski) — hover reveals force diagrams and detail annotations.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes show hover effect (scale 1.05 with 150ms transition)
Styled tooltip appears on hover with node details
Tooltip disappears on mouse leave
Touch devices: tap to toggle tooltip

title: Add gradient fills and subtle depth to node rendering
description:
All nodes are flat colored circles — VA5=1 for most canvases. Flat fill creates 'school project' aesthetic.

Add SVG radialGradient: lighter center (~20% lighter), node color at edge. Leader/active nodes get feGaussianBlur glow (stdDeviation=3, flood-opacity=0.3). Crashed nodes get desaturated gradient. Reference: Shopify BFCM globe uses custom fragment shaders for depth — we need the SVG equivalent. Keep subtle — Linear.app-style restraint, not skeuomorphic.
acceptanceCriteria: All node circles use radial gradient
Leader/active nodes have soft glow filter
Visual hierarchy: leader > active > inactive > crashed
Gradients use theme-aware colors

title: Make all visualizations responsive: mobile 375px to ultra-wide 2560px
description:
VA7=1/3 across all canvases. Fixed viewBox='0 0 600 440' with max-h/max-w constraints. On 375px mobile, 9px SVG text becomes ~5.5px. On 2560px ultra-wide, canvas is a small box.

Fix with useResizeObserver: (1) Mobile (<640px): hide non-essential labels, increase node radius, simplify to essential elements. (2) Tablet (640-1024): standard layout. (3) Desktop (1024-1920): fill container. (4) Ultra-wide (>1920): scale proportionally, don't exceed 1200px width.

Reference: Mike Bostock's visualizations (bost.ocks.org/mike/algorithms/) adapt density based on available space.
acceptanceCriteria: All canvases readable at 375px (min visual text 11px)
All canvases fill container at 1920px+
Labels hide/show based on available space
Node sizes scale for touch targets on mobile

title: Add redistribution animation to Consistent Hashing (keys sliding along ring arc)
description:
Core teaching moment is invisible: when addNode/removeNode is called, keys instantly snap. The '1/N redistribution' property should be VISIBLE. Animate ONLY redistributed keys sliding along the ring perimeter from old owner to new owner. Non-redistributed keys stay still — visual proof of minimal movement.

Reference: selfboot.cn/algorithms/hashring shows real-time color-coded segment updates during redistribution.
acceptanceCriteria: Redistributed keys animate along ring arc to new position
Non-redistributed keys remain stationary
Animation 500-800ms with easeInOut
Count of moved keys displayed during animation
Percentage moved shown (e.g., '34 of 100 keys moved — 34%')

title: Add gossip 'epidemic ripple' animation showing exponential information spread
description:
Gossip arrows are static lines from last step. The core insight — exponential spread — is not visually conveyed. Add ripple: nodes color-transition (gray→blue) when first receiving data. Each round, MORE nodes ripple simultaneously (1→2→4→8). This visual acceleration makes O(log N) TANGIBLE.

Reference: 3Blue1Brown's epidemic visualizations use concentric expanding circles from each infected node. Adapt: expanding color ring from each newly-informed gossip node.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes color-transition when receiving data first time
Ripple count visibly increases each round
Gossip arrows animate sender→receiver with easeOut
Convergence bar animates in sync with ripples

title: Create cinematic Raft leader election animation (the 'screenshot moment')
description:
The Screenshot Test: currently no visualization is share-worthy except CRDT merge.

Design the Raft election as a 2-3 second choreographed sequence:
1. All nodes pulse dim (uncertainty phase)
2. Candidate glows amber, vote-request particles fly to all peers (easeOut, stagger 50ms)
3. Vote-granted particles fly BACK green; vote-denied red
4. Majority: winner BURSTS into leader green with expanding glow ring
5. Heartbeat pulses emanate outward from leader to all followers

Reference: Secret Lives of Data election + Apple WWDC animation quality. Motion Canvas (motioncanvas.io) shows how to choreograph this in TypeScript.
acceptanceCriteria: Election is choreographed 2-3 second sequence
Vote particles fly with easeOut curves
Leader emergence has glow ring burst
Heartbeat pulses visible after election
60fps, no jank

title: Adopt GitLab Pajamas dark-mode color palette for all distributed visualizations
description:
VA1 (Color) scores 1-2 across canvases. Current colors are hardcoded hex with no systematic dark-mode optimization. Research found GitLab Pajamas as the best documented dark-mode data viz system: 5 hues × 11 steps = 55 variables with explicit contrast ratios on dark surfaces.

Adopt their Blue/Aqua/Green/Orange/Magenta categorical palette:
- Blue (primary): 50:#e9ebff → 950:#2a2b59
- Green (success/committed): matching green scale
- Orange (warning/candidate): matching orange scale
- Magenta (conflict/error): matching magenta scale
- Aqua (info/message): matching aqua scale

Dark surface: #18171d (not pure black). Text: #e2e8f0 (not pure white). Grid lines: #475569.

Alternative: Radix UI Colors (radix-ui.com/colors) 30 scales × 12 steps.
acceptanceCriteria: All visualization colors use systematic palette (not ad-hoc hex)
Dark surface background is warm-dark, not pure black
Text uses off-white, not pure white
Categorical colors distinguish 5+ states clearly
All colors meet 3:1 contrast on dark surface (WCAG)

title: Design empty state illustrations for all 11 simulations
description:
Before running, 8 of 11 canvases show either static diagrams (Raft, Gossip, CAP, Consistent Hash) or bare 'Run the simulation to see X' text (2PC, Saga, MapReduce, Paxos). VectorClocks/Lamport show empty space-time lanes.

Design empty states that invite interaction:
- Raft: 5 gray nodes with subtle breathing animation, text 'Click Play to watch a leader election'
- Consistent Hash: Empty ring with pulsing 'Add Node' prompt arrow
- Gossip: Nodes in circle, one highlighted with 'Introduce data to start'
- 2PC/Saga/Paxos: Simplified icon + 'Run to see [protocol name] in action'

Reference: Linear.app empty states — minimal illustration + single clear CTA.
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 simulations have designed empty states
Each has a clear CTA pointing to the first button to press
Empty states use subtle animation (breathing, pulse) not static
Text is action-oriented ('Click Play to...' not 'Run the simulation to see...')

title: Fix all SVG typography: minimum 11px, proper font hierarchy, consistent monospace
description:
VA4 (Typography) scores 1/3 across most canvases. Issues:
- 25+ instances of fontSize='8', '9', '10' (below 11px minimum)
- No font hierarchy: labels, values, and annotations all use similar sizes
- Inconsistent font families: some use fontFamily='monospace', others default SVG sans-serif

Fix: (1) Minimum fontSize='11' everywhere. (2) Hierarchy: node IDs=13px bold, role labels=11px medium, data values=11px monospace, annotations=11px regular. (3) All numeric values use monospace. (4) Consider font-feature-settings='tnum' for tabular numbers.

Reference: NYT Graphics team uses 11px minimum, 3 font-weight levels, strict monospace for data.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero SVG text below 11px
Clear 3-level hierarchy: primary (13px bold), secondary (11px medium), tertiary (11px regular)
All numeric values use monospace font
Labels don't overlap at any supported viewport size

title: Add color-coded ring segments to Consistent Hashing showing ownership arcs
description:
The ConsistentHashCanvas ring (DistributedModule.tsx:438-445) is a plain gray circle. The ownership ranges — which segments of the ring belong to which node — are invisible. The orphaned ConsistentHashRingVisualizer has a comment at line 138 'We skip this for simplicity' about arc segments.

Add colored arc segments: each node's ownership range on the ring should be filled with that node's color (low opacity, ~0.15). When virtual nodes are shown, each vnode's tiny segment should also be colored. This makes the DISTRIBUTION instantly visible — large segments = hot spots, even segments = balanced.

Reference: selfboot.cn/algorithms/hashring shows proportional color-coded ring segments.
acceptanceCriteria: Ring shows colored arc segments per node (using node color at 15% opacity)
Arc sizes visually proportional to owned key range
Segments update when nodes added/removed
Hot spots visible: one large segment = bad distribution

title: Add VectorClock/Lamport event dot entry animation and causal arrow drawing
description:
VectorClockCanvas and LamportCanvas both score F (7/24) on visual quality — the worst in the module. Both are pure static SVG: events pop into existence instantly, arrows appear fully drawn.

Add: (1) Event dots fade-in with scale animation on creation (r: 0→6 over 200ms). (2) Message arrows draw progressively (pathLength animation from 0 to 1). (3) Vector clock labels fade in slightly after dot. Reference: VectorClockDiagram.tsx (orphaned) has motion.circle with initial={{ r: 0 }} animate={{ r: EVENT_DOT_RADIUS }} transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}.

Also add AnimatePresence for proper exit animations when reset is clicked.
acceptanceCriteria: Event dots animate in (scale from 0 to full size)
Message arrows draw progressively (not instant)
Clock/timestamp labels fade in after dot appears
Reset triggers exit animations before clearing

title: Add step transition animations to 2PC, Saga, MapReduce, and Paxos canvases
description:
These 4 step-based simulations have zero transition between steps — when user clicks Step, the SVG state changes instantly. This makes them feel like slideshows, not simulations (SR1 Realism = 1/3 for all four).

Add: (1) 2PC: arrow indicators animate toward participants during prepare, reverse during vote. Phase badge crossfades. (2) Saga: service boxes highlight sequentially with 200ms stagger during forward execution, reverse stagger during compensation. (3) MapReduce: pipeline stages highlight with slide animation as current phase advances. (4) Paxos: message arrows draw progressively between proposers and acceptors.

Reference: Apple Keynote transitions — content slides/fades between states, never pops.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 step-based sims have animated transitions between steps
Phase/stage badges crossfade instead of instant swap
Arrow indicators animate directionally
Transitions are 200-400ms, not blocking user interaction

title: Add partition visualization animation to CAP Theorem canvas
description:
CAPCanvas partition line appears instantly as a static dashed line (DistributedModule.tsx:1570-1590). The SplitBrainVisualizer (Learn panel) has a much better partition: motion.line with initial={{ opacity: 0 }} animate={{ opacity: 0.8 }}, plus partition labels that fade in.

Add: (1) Partition line slides in from edge with 400ms animation. (2) Connection lines between nodes in different partitions fade to red dashed. (3) Partition labels ('Group A' / 'Group B') fade in after line settles. (4) On heal: reverse animation — line slides out, connections restore. (5) Data divergence indicator pulses when detected.

Reference: SplitBrainVisualizer.tsx:287-299 for partition animation pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Partition line animates in (slide + fade, not instant)
Cross-partition links transition to red dashed during partition
Partition labels appear after line animation
Heal reverses: line slides out, links restore
Divergence indicator has pulsing animation

title: Add real-time parameter sliders to Raft and Gossip simulations
description:
SR2 (Parameter Sensitivity) scores 1/3 for Raft and Gossip — both have fixed configs with no user control over key parameters.

Raft: Add sliders for (1) Number of nodes: 3/5/7, (2) Auto-play speed: 50ms-500ms, (3) Election timeout range: adjustable min/max. User should see how changing timeout affects election frequency.

Gossip: Add sliders for (1) Node count: 4-16, (2) Fanout: 1-4, (3) Gossip interval. User should see how fanout affects convergence speed.

Reference: OmniPaxos Playground allows similar parameter tweaking for consensus.
acceptanceCriteria: Raft has node count and speed sliders
Gossip has node count and fanout sliders
Parameters update simulation in real-time (no restart needed for speed)
Sliders show current value and have sensible min/max

title: Add Bartosz Ciechanowski-style interactive cause-and-effect annotations
description:
SR3 (Cause-and-Effect Clarity) averages 1.7/3 — users see WHAT changed but not always WHY. Bartosz Ciechanowski's interactive explainers (ciechanow.ski) set the gold standard: dragging one element shows force arrows and annotations explaining the causal chain.

Adapt for distributed: when an event happens (e.g., election timeout in Raft), draw a brief annotation line from the timer to the action, with a tooltip: 'Timer expired (250ms without heartbeat) → Started election'. Annotations should appear for 2-3 seconds then fade, keeping the canvas clean.

Apply to: Raft (timeout→election, vote→majority→leader), CAP (partition→write rejected), Gossip (version comparison→data exchanged), CRDT (merge→convergence).
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 cause-effect annotations per simulation for top 5 sims
Annotations appear briefly (2-3s) then fade
Annotation connects cause element to effect element visually
Text explains the causal relationship in plain language

title: Add preset scenarios to Raft, CAP, and Gossip (one-click interesting situations)
description:
SR2 (Parameters) is limited because users must discover interesting scenarios themselves. Add preset buttons:

Raft presets: 'Normal Election' (default), 'Split Vote' (forces tie → re-election), 'Leader Crash During Replication' (crash leader after submitting command), 'Network Partition (2|3 split)'

CAP presets: 'Write During Partition (CP vs AP)' (auto: write → partition → write → read), 'Read Your Own Write' (write → immediate read from different node)

Gossip presets: 'Fast Spread (fanout=3)' (introduce data + auto-play), 'Slow Spread (fanout=1)' (same), 'Node Death During Spread' (introduce → kill → watch recovery)

Each preset auto-configures parameters and runs a scripted sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Raft has 4 presets accessible from a dropdown or button group
CAP has 2 presets
Gossip has 3 presets
Each preset auto-runs a scripted scenario
User can pause/modify mid-scenario
